items.note: Title and abstract both state 'cross-sectional' and 'survey-weighted'.
Structured abstract: background, methods, results, conclusion with key numbers.
Background on obesity-diabetes link and rationale for survey-design analysis.
Objective stated: quantify weighted prevalence and demographically adjusted association; association framing.
Cross-sectional design of NHANES 2017-2018 stated early in Methods.
Setting (U.S. non-institutionalized population) and cycle dates (2017-2018) given.
Cross-sectional eligibility criteria given as a numbered list; source = NHANES.
Matching item for cohort/case-control; no matching in this cross-sectional study.
Exposure (obesity BMI>=30), outcome (self-report diabetes), covariates (age, sex, race), and sensitivity definition (HbA1c>=6.5) defined.
Measured BMI from MEC, DIQ010 self-report, LBXGH lab measurement named as data sources.
Some bias handling implicit (HbA1c sensitivity for outcome misclassification, design-based variance); no dedicated 'efforts to address bias' subsection.
No explicit study-size justification; sample is the available NHANES cycle (fixed n). Not addressed as a formal sample-size rationale.
Age handled continuously; BMI dichotomized at 30 with the boundary stated; race as categorical with referent.
Survey-weighted logistic regression with confounder adjustment described.
No subgroup or interaction analyses were performed in this demonstration.
Missing data handled by complete-case eligibility (missing BMI / invalid status excluded; counts reported), but the complete-case strategy is not labeled as such in Methods prose.
Cross-sectional sampling strategy explicitly carried into analysis: weights, strata, PSU, Taylor linearization, design df=15.
HbA1c-based sensitivity analysis described.
Numbers at each eligibility stage reported (9254 -> ... -> 5010).
Reasons for exclusion at each stage given (age, missing BMI, invalid DM status).
STROBE flow diagram provided (Figure 1).
Weighted Table 1 of characteristics by diabetes status; key contrasts in text.
Missing-data counts reported for the exclusion-defining variables (BMI, DM status) in the flow; per-variable missingness in the analytic set not tabulated separately.
Follow-up time item is cohort-specific; cross-sectional study has no follow-up.
Cross-sectional: weighted diabetes prevalence overall and within obesity strata reported (7.6% / 17.3%).
Confounder-adjusted ORs with 95% CIs reported and confounders named; crude/unadjusted OR not reported as a separate estimate (only descriptive weighted prevalences by obesity).
Category boundary for the dichotomized BMI variable (>=30) stated.
Relative-to-absolute over time requires a time period; not meaningful for a cross-sectional prevalence-odds analysis.
Sensitivity analysis (HbA1c outcome) reported in Results.
Key results summarized with reference to the objective (association, ~3-fold odds).
Limitations enumerated with direction of bias (cross-sectional, self-report misclassification, single BMI threshold, residual confounding).
Cautious interpretation: association-only, no causal/prognostic claim.
Generalizability addressed via weighting to the U.S. adult population.
Funding stated (None; methods demonstration) on title page.
